outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase

raw_data.note: Data reported as medians with 95% bootstrap CIs only (non-parametric). CIMT group week 4 median=0.8 m/s (95% bootstrap CI 0.4-1.3); Control group week 4 median=0.7 m/s (95% bootstrap CI 0.4-1.0). Pelvic on group week 4 median=0.7 (0.5-1.7). Pelvic off group week 4 median=0.7 (0.2-1.0). No means or SDs reported.

outcome: Lost to study during intervention phase, dropouts

raw_data.note: 47 patients recruited, 4 lost to follow-up, 43 analyzed. Final groups: pelvic off n=11, pelvic on n=12, CIMT n=10, control n=10. Per-group dropout allocation not specified in text. Total 4 dropouts across all 4 groups. No adverse events reported.

source_quote: We recruited 47 patients, but 4 were lost to follow-up, leaving a cohort of 43 for the final analyses: 10 patients in the control group, 11 in the pelvic off group, 12 in the pelvic on group, and 10 in the CIMT group.

outcome: Walking velocity (metres per second) at end of intervention phase
source_quote: Gait speed (m/s) Group A day 0: 0.93 +/- 0.26, day 14: 1.17 +/- 0.31; Group B day 0: 0.88 +/- 0.28, day 14: 1.03 +/- 0.32; Interaction effect p=0.055

outcome: Lost to study during intervention phase, dropouts
source_quote: Of the 40 participants, three in group A and one in group B dropped out of the trial; two participants in group A retracted informed consent because of mental health issues and one discontinued the trial due to robot-induced skin problems, while one participant in group B had a second stroke attack during the trial period.
